name: MSR SE200 Community Chlorine Maker
description: MSR SE200 Community Chlorine Maker is a portable chlorine generating device which is used for disinfecting water.

taxonomy.sub_category: MSR SE200 Community Chlorine Maker Updated on January 3, 2024 · Created on September 27, 2018 MSR SE200 Community Chlorine Maker MSR SE200 Community Chlorine Maker is a portable chlorine generating device which is used for disinfecting water. Developed By Cascade Designers, Inc Tested By Antenna Technologies Mercy Corps World Vision Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description MSR SE220 Community Chlorine Maker is a device that can generate up to 200 litres of chlorine solution in just 5 mins with the help of only salt, water, and electricity. The product is commercially available and is manufactured by Seattle-based outdoor company Mountain Safety Research(MSR) and PATH, a global health NGO remote for the functioning. The portable disinfection unit is effective in pathogen removal (bacteria, virus, protozoa). Target SDGs SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation SDG 3: Good Health and Well-Being Market Suggested Retail Price $249.95 Target Users (Target Impact Group) Household, Community, Public Sector Agencies, NGOs Distributors / Implementing Organizations This product is implemented by Mountain Safety Research (MSR) and other implementing partners such as World Vision, Mercy Crops, Antenna technologies, and Operation Blessing. Competitive Landscape Direct competitors include PurAll Water Purification , P&G Purifier of Water , WATA Products , Dispensers for Safe Water , Lotus Water Chlorine Dosing Device , Zimba automatic chlorine dispenser , Aquatab Tablets , and WaterGuard . Regions Worldwide Manufacturing/Building Method The product is commercially manufactured in the US by Cascade Designs Inc., but some parts are imported. Intellectural Property Type Patent User Provision Model The product can be purchased directly through MSR or through a few eCommerce markets like amazon and REI Co-Op. Distributions to Date Status As of October 2017, 1500 devices sold or distributed globally. Active Chemical and concentration (%) Chlorine 0.8% Form Liquid Dosing method: Batch or flow-through Batch Dosing method: Automatic or manual Manual Dosing quantity Unknown Contact time (min) 5 min Target free disinfectant residual (mg/L) Unknown Bacteria reduction Effective against bacteria removal Virus reduction Effective against Virus removal Protozoa reduction Effective against Protozoa removal Heavy metals and/or arsenic reduction No reduction Treatment lifespan (days) 24 hours Maximum recommended influent turbidity level (NTU) Not applicable Design Specifications This product works on Electrochlorination technology. Users mix salt brine, add it to the chlorine maker and push the start button. Indicator lights and sounds signal a successful completion or troubleshooting. The product requires a 12-V battery. Accessories– Chlorine Residual Test Strips, Chlorine Maker Power Supply Kit, Chlorine Maker 12V Auto Adaptor Product Schematics Technical Support Instruction Manual provided by MSR. Replacement Components Replaceable components include the Salt, Chlorine Residual Test Strips, Sample Bottle, Chlorine Maker Power Supply Kit, and Chlorine Maker 12V Auto Adaptor. Lifecycle The product comes with a limited warranty but can run for a lifetime of 5+ years. Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters Designer specified performance targets include lightweight, durable, portable, and locally available. Vetted Performance Status Validated by end-users in over a dozen low-resource settings, including communities in Kenya, Mali, Tanzania, Zimbabwe, and Thailand. Safety Users must have caution when working with electricity near water. Complementary Technical Systems Safe water storage units and water filtration units. Available: https://media.path.org/documents/TS_update_se200_2015.pdf Compliance with regulations Unknown Evaluation methods 30 households were selected for testing in Kenya in 2010. A study was conducted on them determining the water behavior, norms, and water quality of the water. Water quality was analyzed before and after adding chlorine from MSR SE200.
